faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subheading: Everything you need to know about cold pressed oils and how we work.

items.a: Cold pressing extracts oil using only mechanical pressure, keeping temperatures below 40°C throughout. This preserves the natural vitamins, antioxidants, and flavour that are destroyed when heat or chemicals are used in industrial extraction.
We bring 40+ years of expertise in the craft and combine it with the perfect Sun-dry weather of Challakere to deliver nutrition rich cold pressed oils straight to your door.

items.q: How is Chetana oil different from supermarket oils?
items.a: Supermarket oils are typically extracted using hexane (a petroleum solvent) at temperatures above 150°C, then bleached and deodorised. Chetana oils are single-ingredient — just the seed, cold pressed. Nothing added, nothing removed.

We take orders via WhatsApp. Just tap the 'Order via WhatsApp' button, send us your requirement, and we'll confirm availability and delivery details. We deliver within Challakere and surrounding areas.

items.q: How long does the oil stay fresh?
items.a: Cold pressed oils have a natural shelf life of 6 months when stored in a cool, dark place. Unlike refined oils, ours have no preservatives — so proper storage matters. Keep the cap tight and away from direct sunlight.

items.q: Can I use cold pressed oil for high-heat cooking and deep frying?
items.a: Absolutely. You can get more done with lesser amount of oil, so we suggest experimenting with portions if you are new to cold pressed oils.

Yes. We source seeds directly from farmers in and around Challakere, Chitradurga district, Karnataka. This keeps quality consistent and supports local agriculture. For some varieties, we work with the same farming families every season.
